meta.description: System design interview questions commonly associated with Amazon SDE interviews

questions.title: Design Amazon.com Product Page
questions.description: Design the product detail page on Amazon.com that displays product information, pricing, reviews, availability, and seller offers with high availability during peak traffic like Prime Day.
key_discussion_points: Product catalog storage and schema (billions of SKUs)
Dynamic pricing and offer selection (buy box algorithm)
Customer reviews and ratings aggregation
Real-time inventory and availability checks
Personalization: related products, frequently bought together
Image and media asset serving via CDN
Caching strategy for product data (multi-tier cache)
Handling traffic spikes during sales events (Prime Day, Black Friday)

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_I: Core page data model, API design for product info retrieval, basic caching, CDN for images
expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: Multi-tier caching, buy box logic overview, review aggregation pipeline, inventory check integration, handling traffic spikes
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Microservice decomposition, global catalog consistency, personalization integration, graceful degradation under extreme load, platform-level caching infrastructure

questions.title: Design an Order Processing System
questions.description: Design Amazon's order processing pipeline from checkout through payment, fulfillment, shipping, and delivery tracking.
key_discussion_points: Order state machine (placed, confirmed, picking, packing, shipped, delivered)
Payment processing and fraud detection integration
Inventory reservation and decrement (avoiding overselling)
Distributed transactions across microservices (saga pattern)
Fulfillment center selection and routing optimization
Event-driven architecture for order lifecycle events
Retry and idempotency for payment and inventory operations
Order tracking and customer notification pipeline

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: Order state machine, basic saga pattern, payment integration, inventory reservation, event-driven notifications
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Distributed saga orchestration, idempotency design, multi-warehouse fulfillment optimization, exactly-once payment processing, monitoring and alerting
expected_depth_by_level.Principal_SDE: Cross-region order processing, global consistency model, fulfillment network optimization, platform-level reliability guarantees, cost optimization across the pipeline

questions.title: Design AWS S3
questions.description: Design a highly durable, available, and scalable object storage service that stores trillions of objects.
key_discussion_points: Object storage API: PUT, GET, DELETE, LIST with bucket namespace
Data durability: replication across availability zones (11 nines)
Storage node architecture and data placement
Metadata management at massive scale
Consistency model: strong read-after-write consistency
Storage classes and lifecycle policies (Standard, IA, Glacier)
Multipart upload for large objects
Access control: bucket policies, IAM, pre-signed URLs

expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Core object storage architecture, replication for durability, metadata indexing, consistency model, multi-part upload, access control
expected_depth_by_level.Principal_SDE: Cross-AZ and cross-region replication, erasure coding vs replication trade-offs, storage class tiering with lifecycle transitions, metadata sharding at trillion-object scale, cost-per-GB optimization

questions.title: Design a Recommendation Engine for Amazon
questions.description: Design the system that powers product recommendations across Amazon including 'Customers who bought this also bought', 'Recommended for you', and personalized homepage.
key_discussion_points: Collaborative filtering: item-to-item and user-to-user approaches
Content-based filtering using product attributes
Two-stage architecture: candidate generation and ranking
Real-time signals: recent views, searches, cart additions
Batch computation of recommendation models (item similarity matrices)
A/B testing framework for recommendation algorithms
Cold start handling for new users and new products
Serving architecture: pre-computed vs real-time recommendations

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_I: Collaborative filtering basics, item similarity computation, pre-computed recommendation lists, basic API design
expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: Two-stage retrieval and ranking, real-time signal integration, cold start strategies, A/B testing, caching recommendations
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Embedding-based retrieval with ANN, multi-objective ranking, personalization at scale, feedback loop mitigation, platform-level recommendation infrastructure

questions.title: Design Alexa Voice Assistant Backend
questions.description: Design the backend system for a voice assistant that handles speech recognition, natural language understanding, skill routing, and response generation.
key_discussion_points: Audio streaming and speech-to-text (ASR) pipeline
Natural language understanding: intent classification and entity extraction
Skill routing and third-party skill integration
Dialog management and context tracking across turns
Text-to-speech (TTS) response generation
Wake word detection (on-device vs cloud)
Latency budget: end-to-end response within 1-2 seconds
Smart home device integration and IoT protocol handling

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: Audio streaming pipeline, ASR service integration, intent classification, skill routing, TTS response, basic dialog state
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Multi-turn dialog management, low-latency pipeline optimization, third-party skill platform design, smart home protocol bridging, on-device vs cloud partitioning
expected_depth_by_level.Principal_SDE: End-to-end latency optimization across the full pipeline, multi-language and multi-region deployment, skill platform scalability, proactive assistance and context-aware responses, privacy architecture

questions.title: Design a Delivery Routing System
questions.description: Design a system that optimizes delivery routes for Amazon's last-mile delivery fleet, minimizing delivery time and cost while handling real-time constraints.
key_discussion_points: Vehicle Routing Problem (VRP) with time windows and capacity constraints
Route optimization algorithms: heuristic approaches for NP-hard problem
Real-time rerouting based on traffic, weather, and delivery failures
Driver assignment and workload balancing
Delivery time window promises and SLA management
Integration with mapping and traffic data services
Package grouping and load optimization
Tracking and real-time delivery status updates

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: VRP formulation, basic routing heuristics, driver assignment, time window constraints, tracking pipeline
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Real-time rerouting with dynamic constraints, multi-depot optimization, delivery promise accuracy, fleet utilization metrics, integration with warehouse dispatch

questions.title: Design Amazon Prime Video Streaming
questions.description: Design a video streaming platform that delivers on-demand and live content to millions of concurrent viewers with adaptive quality across devices.
key_discussion_points: Video ingestion and transcoding pipeline (multiple codecs, resolutions, bitrates)
Content storage and CDN distribution strategy
Adaptive bitrate streaming (ABR) with DASH/HLS
DRM and content protection across devices
Playback session management and resume functionality
Live streaming architecture with low latency
Content catalog and metadata management
Personalized content recommendations and continue-watching

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: Transcoding pipeline, CDN basics, ABR streaming, DRM overview, playback session state, catalog API
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Multi-codec strategy (AV1, H.265, H.264), CDN origin-shield architecture, per-title encoding optimization, live streaming ingest, device-specific packaging
expected_depth_by_level.Principal_SDE: Global CDN placement and capacity planning, cost-per-stream optimization, real-time quality of experience monitoring, platform convergence for VOD and live, multi-region content licensing enforcement

questions.title: Design an Inventory Management System
questions.description: Design an inventory management system that tracks stock across hundreds of fulfillment centers, supports real-time availability checks, and handles inventory replenishment.
key_discussion_points: Inventory data model: SKU, location, quantity, reservation state
Real-time stock level tracking across warehouses
Inventory reservation during checkout (soft lock with TTL)
Consistency vs availability trade-off for stock counts
Demand forecasting and automated replenishment triggers
Multi-warehouse allocation and transfer optimization
Handling overselling and backorder scenarios
Integration with supplier systems and purchase order management

expected_depth_by_level.SDE_I: Core data model, CRUD APIs for inventory operations, basic reservation with TTL, stock level queries
expected_depth_by_level.SDE_II: Distributed inventory across warehouses, reservation consistency, demand-based replenishment triggers, handling race conditions, event-driven stock updates
expected_depth_by_level.Senior_SDE: Global inventory visibility with eventual consistency, multi-warehouse allocation optimization, forecasting integration, oversell mitigation strategies, audit trail and reconciliation
